title: plectronia cuspidata
intro: Plectronia cuspidata, a tropical plant species native to the Philippines and other parts of Southeast Asia, belongs to the Rubiaceae family, commonly known as the coffee or madder family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. Its health benefits are attributed to the presence of bioactive compounds that can help reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and protect against infections. Plectronia cuspidata is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion, often mixed with other herbs, to create teas or tonics for its medicinal uses.
